meta.reasoning: As evil leader, proposing myself + a good player (s2). Plan to play success to build trust as a credible leader. Keeping s4 hidden for later quests. Quest 3 (size 2) is the key window to strike — if I can get myself or s4 on that team, we can sabotage then.
I am Good and I'm on the proposed team. Approving is safe from my side. If the quest fails, s1 is confirmed Evil — useful early information. Rejecting gains nothing and wastes the rejection counter.

Quest 1, no prior information. Approving gathers data — if quest fails, we know s1 or s2 is Evil. Rejecting wastes a rejection count with zero information gain. Standard Good strategy: let the first quest run to collect evidence.
Team [s1, s2] includes my Evil ally s1. Approving lets s1 execute the sabotage. 3 approvals already in — proposal passes either way. Blending in as neutral Good player.
First quest, no information to go on. Approving to gather data — if the quest fails with s1+s2, we know one of them is Evil. All other players already approved, so the outcome is decided regardless.
